content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Yes, both touch encode() in core.py - unavoidable overlap. Suggestion to minimize merge pain: each of us adds our new kwarg at the END of the encode() signature (after existing params), and puts our logic as an early, self-contained branch near the top of the method body (e.g. 'if return_positions: ...return...' as the first check), rather than interleaving with the core encode logic. That way our diffs touch different regions and a merge conflict, if any, will be trivial to resolve by keeping both branches. I'll do this for return_positions. Proceeding to implement now.
